stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face.
It is categorized within the text-generation pipeline, indicating its primary function.
The associated tags confirm its relevance to transformers, Llama models, and the AI ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its use in large language model applications.
The model is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: Google publishes exploit code threatening millions of Chromium users
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Google disclosed exploit code affecting millions of Chromium users.
The vulnerability was reported nearly 29 months prior to the public disclosure.
The exploit code was published before the corresponding patch was widely available.

stories.title: Buckle up: Google is set to remake search with agentic AI in 2026
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is planning a major overhaul of its search engine.
The new iteration will be driven by 'agentic AI'.
The rollout or significant unveiling is anticipated around I/O 2026.

stories.title: Toto 2.0: Time Series Forecasting Enters the Scaling Era
stories.author: David Asker
bullet_points: Introduces Toto 2.0, a new family of open-weights time series foundation models.
Demonstrates that time series models scale effectively, showing reliable forecast improvements across different parameter sizes.
Achieves state-of-the-art performance on three key forecasting benchmarks (BOOM, GIFT-Eval, and TIME).

stories.title: GitHub confirms breach of 3,800 repos via malicious VSCode extension
stories.author: Timofeibu
bullet_points: GitHub confirmed a security breach affecting 3,800 repositories.
The vector for the breach was identified as a malicious VSCode extension.
This highlights critical supply chain and extension security risks in the developer ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a specific model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, trending on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text generation and is associated with the deepseek_v3 architecture.
It is relevant to the broader AI/ML community due to its presence in the transformers ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for performance enhancement.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing AI workloads for macOS users.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy and control.
The core value proposition is connecting communication tools to local, private AI capabilities.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It allows running advanced coding models (like Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex) locally or in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models are available in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are permissively licensed under Apache 2.0.
This partnership with OpenAI and ROOST aims to bring advanced safety tools to the open-source community.

stories.title: You can now remix other people’s YouTube Shorts with AI
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Google has introduced a new YouTube Shorts Remix feature powered by Gemini Omni.
This feature allows users to restyle existing Shorts or insert themselves into other creators' videos using AI.
The functionality is accessible via a 'reimagine' option when clicking the remix icon on a Short.

stories.title: Google Search’s AI evolution includes more ads
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Google is integrating its Gemini AI model directly into Google Search results.
The AI feature will generate 'custom explainers' to persuade users to purchase specific products.
This marks an evolution where AI capabilities are being leveraged to enhance and monetize the search experience.

stories.title: When Does Model Collapse Occur in Structured Interactive Learning?
stories.author: Weijie Su
bullet_points: The rise of generative AI creates interactive learning environments where models are updated using synthetic data from other models.
This paradigm challenges classical statistical learning by meaning training data is no longer exclusively from the target population.
The paper investigates 'Model Collapse' within these structured, interactive learning settings.

stories.title: TIDE: Efficient and Lossless MoE Diffusion LLM Inference with I/O-aware Expert Offload
stories.author: Yuzhang Shang
bullet_points: Addresses the deployment challenge of large Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) Diffusion Large Language Models (dLLMs) on resource-constrained devices.
Introduces TIDE, a method designed for efficient and lossless inference by incorporating I/O-aware expert offloading.
Contributes to the growing field of dLLMs as a competitive alternative to traditional autoregressive (AR) models.

stories.title: From Seeing to Thinking: Decoupling Perception and Reasoning Improves Post-Training of Vision-Language Models
stories.author: Yuyin Zhou
bullet_points: The research addresses a key limitation in Vision-Language Models (VLMs): visual perception often bottlenecks performance more than reasoning ability.
The study proposes decoupling VLM capabilities into three distinct training stages: visual perception, visual reasoning, and textual reasoning.
This modular approach aims to improve VLM post-training by specializing the training for each component.

stories.title: ClinSeekAgent: Automating Multimodal Evidence Seeking for Agentic Clinical Reasoning
stories.author: Yuyin Zhou
bullet_points: Introduces ClinSeekAgent, a framework designed to automate dynamic, multimodal evidence seeking for clinical reasoning.
Addresses a key limitation in current LLM applications in medicine: the assumption that evidence is pre-curated.
Shifts the paradigm from passive information consumption to active, iterative evidence planning and synthesis in clinical workflows.

stories.title: ThoughtTrace: Understanding User Thoughts in Real-World LLM Interactions
stories.author: Tianmin Shu
bullet_points: Introduces ThoughtTrace, a novel, large-scale dataset designed to capture users' internal thoughts during LLM interactions.
The dataset pairs real-world multi-turn conversations with self-reported user motivations (reasons for prompts) and reactions to AI responses.
It spans 2,155 conversations across 20 different language models, addressing a critical gap in current conversational AI research.

stories.title: Google I/O Review (4/5) — Google Quietly Killed Gemini CLI
stories.author: ww-w-ai
bullet_points: Google announced the discontinuation of the Gemini CLI, shifting it from Apache 2.0 to closed source.
The change significantly restricts usage, limiting free requests to 1,000 per day.
This development contrasts with positive announcements from Google I/O 2026 regarding Flash benchmarks and serverless agents.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The associated pipeline is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on generative AI tasks.
Key technologies involved include transformers, PyTorch, and safetensors.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental local image generation capabilities on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local hardware.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code capabilities within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local models and proprietary models like Claude.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a working directory.
Users can leverage various open-weight alternatives like gpt-oss:20b or gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Samsung has a tentative deal with workers to avoid a memory chip strike
stories.author: Jess Weatherbed
bullet_points: Samsung avoided a potential 18-day strike involving over 47,000 workers.
The dispute centered on collapsed bonus payment negotiations between the company and its union.
The averted strike was significant given current global constraints and shortages in memory chip production.

stories.title: Chickens without eggs? De-extinction company creates artificial egg.
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: Colossal Biosciences has developed a method to create artificial eggs.
This development is linked to their work in de-extinction projects.
The technology may offer a valuable tool for broader developmental biology research.

stories.title: Yearslong fight over users' right to tweak smart TV software heads to trial
stories.author: Scharon Harding
bullet_points: A protracted legal battle is moving to trial regarding users' rights to modify smart TV operating system software.
The core issue revolves around whether users should have access to the TV OS source code.
Granting source code access could empower users to mitigate advertising and tracking practices.

stories.title: A Methodology for Selecting and Composing Runtime Architecture Patterns for Production LLM Agents
stories.author: Vasundra Srinivasan
bullet_points: Introduces the 'stochastic-deterministic boundary' (SDB) as a critical architectural concept for production LLM agents.
The SDB defines a four-part contract (proposer, verifier, commit, reject) governing how LLM outputs translate into system actions.
Argues that the SDB is the fundamental, load-bearing primitive for designing robust LLM agent runtimes.

stories.title: HaorFloodAlert: Deseasonalized ML Ensemble for 72-Hour Flood Prediction in Bangladesh Haor Wetlands
stories.author: Md. Zakir Hossen
bullet_points: Introduces HaorFloodAlert, a new ML ensemble designed for 72-hour flood prediction in Bangladesh's Haor wetlands.
Addresses a critical gap in current flood prediction models, which are designed for riverine systems and fail to capture backwater dynamics in flat basins.
The model specifically tackles the issue of seasonality (temperature bias) that previously degraded prediction accuracy.

stories.title: Not Every Rubric Teaches Equally: Policy-Aware Rubric Rewards for RLVR
stories.author: Yunzhong He
bullet_points: The research addresses a limitation in rubric-based rewards for Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR).
Standard rubric aggregation conflates a criterion's inherent human importance with its actual utility as an optimization signal.
The paper proposes a novel 'Policy-Aware Rubric' to better balance qualitative criteria during training.

stories.title: Rethinking Visual Attribution for Chest X-ray Reasoning in Large Vision Language Models
stories.author: Aidong Zhang
bullet_points: Addresses the critical issue of visual attribution reliability in Large Vision Language Models (LVLMs) used for medical imaging (Chest X-rays).
Highlights the trustworthiness gap: current attribution methods may not accurately reflect the visual evidence driving the model's clinical decisions.
Focuses on the challenge of verifying internal model reasoning when ground-truth annotations are unavailable.

stories.title: Multi-axis Analysis of Image Manipulation Localization
stories.author: Bryan A. Plummer
bullet_points: The proliferation of generative AI has made advanced image manipulation highly accessible.
Manipulated images pose a growing threat due to their potential to spread misinformation and influence public opinion.
The research addresses a gap in detecting advanced manipulations across various visual domains.

stories.title: HaorFloodAlert: Deseasonalized ML Ensemble for 72-Hour Flood Prediction in Bangladesh Haor Wetlands
stories.author: Md. Zakir Hossen
bullet_points: Introduces HaorFloodAlert, a new ML ensemble designed for 72-hour flood prediction in Bangladesh's Haor wetlands.
Addresses a critical gap in current flood prediction models by accounting for unique backwater dynamics in flat basins, unlike traditional riverine models.
The research specifically tackles the issue of seasonality (temperature bias) that previously degraded prediction accuracy.

stories.title: Interpretable Computer Vision for Defect Detection in X-ray Tomography of Aerospace SiC/SiC Composites
stories.author: Thomas Philippe
bullet_points: Addresses the 'black-box' problem in deep learning applications for industrial inspection.
Focuses on improving defect detection in X-ray Computed Tomography (XCT) of aerospace SiC/SiC composites.
Introduces p-ResNet-50, a novel convolutional framework designed to provide interpretable computer vision for quality control.

stories.title: SAGE: Scalable Automatic Gating Ensemble for Confident Negative Harvesting in Fraud Detection
stories.author: Amit Goyal
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of music streaming fraud, where artificial stream inflation manipulates royalties.
Introduces SAGE, a novel counterfactual-aware negative harvesting ensemble for detection.
Tackles the difficulty of distinguishing sophisticated fraud from legitimate user behavior (e.g., super-fans).

stories.title: KoRe: Compact Knowledge Representations for Large Language Models
stories.author: Jacopo Staiano
bullet_points: The paper addresses the inherent flaw in LLMs encoding world knowledge solely within opaque parameters.
It proposes 'KoRe,' a method for creating Compact Knowledge Representations to improve LLM knowledge handling.
The research contrasts LLM parameter-based knowledge with the human-readable structure of Knowledge Graphs.

stories.title: Rethinking Visual Attribution for Chest X-ray Reasoning in Large Vision Language Models
stories.author: Aidong Zhang
bullet_points: LVLMs are promising in medical imaging (Chest X-rays), but their lack of reliable visual grounding is a major clinical trust issue.
Current visual attribution methods used to explain LVLM decisions are often unverified regarding their true link to the visual evidence.
The research addresses the challenge of verifying internal model reasoning when ground-truth annotations are unavailable.

stories.title: Less Back-and-Forth: A Comparative Study of Structured Prompting
stories.author: Abdou Sow
bullet_points: The study investigates how structured prompting techniques (checklist vs. clarifying questions) can improve LLM response quality.
It compares three prompt conditions against four diverse task types: summarization, planning, explanation, and coding.
The core finding addresses the inefficiency of underspecified prompts by aiming to reduce user interaction while boosting output quality.

stories.title: MixRea: Benchmarking Explicit-Implicit Reasoning in Large Language Models
stories.author: Yanru Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces 'MixRea' to benchmark explicit-implicit reasoning capabilities in Large Language Models (LLMs).
Investigates if LLMs suffer from a form of 'inattentional blindness' when following explicit instructions.
Addresses the growing reliance on LLMs in high-stakes decision-making by testing attentional biases.

stories.title: BalanceRAG: Joint Risk Calibration for Cascaded Retrieval-Augmented Generation
stories.author: Zhiyuan Wang
bullet_points: Introduces BalanceRAG, a method for cascaded Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG).
The system uses an LLM-only branch first, escalating to RAG only when the primary model is uncertain.
It aims to improve factuality while avoiding unnecessary RAG calls, addressing calibration issues in cascaded systems.

stories.title: CopT: Contrastive On-Policy Thinking with Continuous Spaces for General and Agentic Reasoning
stories.author: Wenke Lee
bullet_points: Introduces CopT, a new reasoning pipeline designed to improve upon standard Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting in LLMs.
Addresses the inefficiency of CoT by allowing for 'performative reasoning'—providing answers before extended thinking is complete.
CopT utilizes a contrastive, on-policy approach with continuous spaces to enhance general and agentic reasoning capabilities.

stories.title: GitHub Source Code Breach - TeamPCP Claims Access to Internal Source Code
stories.author: cybersecuritynews.com via mseri
bullet_points: A source code breach involving GitHub has been reported.
The group TeamPCP claims to have gained access to internal source code.
The news is being discussed on the Lobsters forum, indicating community interest in the security incident.

stories.title: Per-User OAuth for AI Agents: Why It Matters and What to Look For
stories.author: dumebii
bullet_points: The article discusses the necessity of per-user OAuth specifically for AI agents.
It highlights that AI agents introduce new complexities beyond traditional software interactions.
The topic focuses on security and access control as AI agents integrate with personal data sources (e.g., Slack).

stories.title: The Hidden Networking Problem Behind AI Agent Failures
stories.author: mournfulcord
bullet_points: AI agent development often assumes an idealized, perfect network environment.
The article highlights that real-world network imperfections are a significant, often overlooked, factor in AI agent failures.
This points to a need to account for latency, loss, and imperfections when building robust AI systems.

stories.title: AI-Written Code Is Only Better When a Skilled Programmer Is Holding the Wheel
stories.author: fatihsennik
bullet_points: The article argues that AI-generated code's effectiveness is heavily dependent on the skill level of the human programmer.
It suggests that AI tools are best utilized as assistants rather than complete replacements for expert developers.
The piece touches upon the ongoing debate regarding the role of human expertise in the age of advanced coding AI.
